name: pyjon-utils

metadata.name: pyjon.utils
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Useful tools library with classes to do singletons, dynamic function pointers...

documentation:
Introduction
============

Useful tools library with classes to do singletons, dynamic function pointers...

Python 3 support
================

We have just added python 3 support, but the Singleton metaclass does
 not work yet. You should not use the Singleton with python3

modules.name: pyjon.utils.base_converter
documentation:
Convert numbers from any base to any other base.

This is based on the work by wanyewon (PSF license):
    http://code.activestate.com/recipes/65212/#c9

functions.name: pyjon.utils.base_converter.decimal_to_n
pyjon.utils.base_converter.n_to_m
pyjon.utils.main.get_secure_filename
documentation:
creates a tempfile in the most secure manner possible,
make sure is it closed and return the filename for
easy usage.

functions.name: pyjon.utils.main.create_function
documentation:
This is an utility function that creates functions based on others.

Examples :

```pycon
>>> range_5 = create_function(range, args=[5])
>>> range_5()
[0, 1, 2, 3, 4]
```

```pycon
>>> def argument_returner(*args):
...     ''' a function that just returns its args as a list '''
...     return list(args)
>>> my_func = create_function(argument_returner, args=[1, '2'])
>>> my_func()
[1, '2']
```

```pycon
>>> my_other_func = create_function(argument_returner, args=[1, '2'],
...                                 caller_args_count=2)
>>> my_other_func()
Traceback (most recent call last):
    ...
TypeError: function takes exactly 2 arguments (0 given)
```

```pycon
>>> my_other_func('foo', 'bar')
['foo', 'bar', 1, '2']
```

functions.name: pyjon.utils.main.substitute
documentation:
Substitute keywords in a string using a callback function.
Removes undefined keywords.

```
param string

the string in which keywords must be replaced

param callback

a keyword will be replaced by the return value of
callback(keyword)

param default

when the callback returns None, replace by default instead

param regex

the regex used to find the keywords

param sub_if_none

if True, try substituting by the default value when the
callback returns None
```

Examples :

```pycon
>>> s = "foo {key1} baz {key2}"
>>> d = dict(key1="bar", key2="toto", key3="plouf")
>>> substitute(s, d.get)
'foo bar baz toto'
```

classes.name: pyjon.utils.main.Singleton
documentation:
This is a neat singleton pattern. This was found in

: a comment on this page:

<http://www.garyrobinson.net/2004/03/python_singleto.html>

to use this, example :
\>>> class C(object):
... \_\_metaclass\_\_ = Singleton
... def \_\_init\_\_(self, foo):
... self.foo = foo

```pycon
>>> C('bar').foo
'bar'
```

```pycon
>>> C().foo
'bar'
```

and your class C is now a singleton, and it is safe to use the

: \_\_init\_\_ method as you usually do...
